privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic but realistic insurance plan data based on ACA metal tier standards. The three plans (Bronze, Silver, Gold) have coinsurance rates of 40%, 30%, and 20% respectively, which exactly match ACA specifications. Deductibles ($6,000 Bronze, $3,500 Silver, $1,500 Gold) and out-of-pocket maximums ($8,500, $6,000, $4,000) are realistic and below 2025-2026 federal maximums. Monthly premiums ($280, $380, $480) create appropriate trade-offs where lower premiums correlate with higher patient cost-sharing. The cost calculation logic correctly implements insurance mechanics: patients pay all medical costs up to their deductible, then a percentage (coinsurance) of remaining costs, with a cap at the out-of-pocket maximum. Mathematical verification confirms the expected cost patterns: Bronze is cheapest under low usage ($2,000), Gold is cheapest under high usage ($25,000), and the crossover demonstrates realistic insurance trade-offs.
